title: T-011 — Warehouse indexes (FEFO composite, ltree GiST, genealogy, stock_moves) (WH §5.2, §5.3, §5.6, §8.6)
prompt:
# T-011 — Warehouse indexes (FEFO composite, ltree GiST, genealogy, stock_moves) (WH §5.2, §5.3, §5.6, §8.6)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §5.2, §5.3, §5.6, §8.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add the four index families required to meet §11.2 traceability <30 s, §9.2 FEFO <500 ms, and §8.6 ltree zone roll-up SLOs across license_plates, lp_genealogy, stock_moves, and locations.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `0NN_warehouse_indexes.sql` adding (if not already present): (1) `license_plates(org_id, warehouse_id, product_id, status, expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST)` composite; (2) `locations` GiST index on `path ltree` column; (3) `lp_genealogy(parent_lp_id, child_lp_id)` composite + the two single-col indexes per §5.3; (4) `stock_moves` partial indexes from §5.6.
2. Index naming follows `idx_<table>_<purpose>` convention.
3. Use `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` in production migrations (dev migrations may opt out and document the reason).

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_warehouse_indexes.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/__tests__/warehouse-indexes.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when `pg_indexes` is queried, then all four index families exist with expected column lists.
2. Given §9.2 FEFO query runs against a 100k-row seeded license_plates, when EXPLAIN ANALYZE inspects the plan, then it uses the composite index (no Seq Scan).
3. Given an ltree `path @> 'apex.wh_a'::ltree` query runs, when EXPLAIN inspects the plan, then it uses the GiST index.

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/migrations/__tests__/warehouse-indexes.test.ts` asserting each index exists + EXPLAIN uses it.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test warehouse-indexes`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not drop existing indexes without `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` replacement.
- Do not skip the ltree GiST — zone roll-up dashboard would do Seq Scan over locations.

## Dependencies
- T-002
- T-003
- T-006

pipeline_inputs.details: Add the four index families required to meet §11.2 traceability <30 s, §9.2 FEFO <500 ms, and §8.6 ltree zone roll-up SLOs across license_plates, lp_genealogy, stock_moves, and locations. Migration `0NN_warehouse_indexes.sql` adding (if not already present): (1) `license_plates(org_id, warehouse_id, product_id, status, expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST)` composite; (2) `locations` GiST index on `path ltree` column; (3) `lp_genealogy(parent_lp_id, child_lp_id)` composite + the two single-col indexes per §5.3; (4) `stock_moves` partial indexes from §5.6. Index naming follows `idx_<table>_<purpose>` convention. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-tenant-context-remediation.md`).
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration runs, when `pg_indexes` is queried, then all four index families exist with expected column lists.
Given §9.2 FEFO query runs against a 100k-row seeded license_plates, when EXPLAIN ANALYZE inspects the plan, then it uses the composite index (no Seq Scan).
Given an ltree `path @> 'apex.wh_a'::ltree` query runs, when EXPLAIN inspects the plan, then it uses the GiST index.
test_strategy: RED: `packages/db/migrations/__tests__/warehouse-indexes.test.ts` asserting each index exists + EXPLAIN uses it.
Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test warehouse-indexes`.
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not use tenant_id as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is org_id. See _meta/audits/2026-05-14-tenant-context-remediation.md.
Do not drop existing indexes without `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` replacement.
Do not skip the ltree GiST — zone roll-up dashboard would do Seq Scan over locations.

PO/TO/WO read models, RM root reservations, WO release/cancel hooks
LP lookup, barcode validation, lock protocol, FEFO suggestion, consume-to-WO
WO material consumption and output LP creation events
